headline: CATTLE BREAK $252 AS OIL SURGES 3%
subheadline: Live cattle crashed through Friday's floor while crude oil spiked on supply concerns.
lead: Live cattle closed $253.00, down 98 cents and decisively below the $252 floor that was supposed to hold the breakout story together. The break happened with volume and conviction — not the drift we've been watching, but actual selling. Friday's call on holding pricing discipline above $252 didn't make it through the weekend. The breakout thesis is off the table.
the_takeaway: Cattle breakout is dead; oil spike adds inflation pressure to feed costs.
teaser: Cattle crash through key support while oil spikes 3% on supply fears.

one_number.context: Down 98 cents and decisively through the $252 support level that was supposed to validate the breakout attempt. When a binary level breaks with volume, the story changes fast. The funds that were testing the upside are now testing the downside.

yesterdays_call.summary: Hold pricing discipline above $252 — below that level questions the breakout thesis.
yesterdays_call.note: The break was clean and decisive, not the slow drift we expected.

sections.title: Cattle Break Support
sections.body: Live cattle closed $253.00, down $0.98 and through the $252 level that defined the breakout attempt. This wasn't drift — this was selling with conviction. Feeder cattle followed at $372.18, down 0.4%, confirming the livestock complex is repricing lower. The managed money positioning that built during the attempted breakout is now working against the market. Box beef cutout hasn't provided any support signals.
sections.bottom_line: Binary support level failed; breakout thesis invalidated.
sections.farmer_action: Lock remaining cattle above $250 — next support isn't until $245.
sections.vs_yesterday: Cattle: Friday's floor didn't hold through the weekend.

sections.title: Oil Spike Pressures Costs
sections.body: WTI crude surged $3.38 to $105.19, the biggest single-day move in three weeks on supply disruption concerns. Natural gas eased $0.02 to $2.81, but the oil spike is what matters for diesel and fertilizer input costs. The energy complex is adding inflationary pressure exactly when livestock margins were already compressed. Corn gained 1½ cents to $4.80 but the energy story is bigger than the grain story today.
sections.bottom_line: Oil surge adds cost pressure when margins can't absorb it.

sections.title: Grains Hold Pattern
sections.body: Corn closed $4.80, up 1½ cents in sympathy with energy but still range-bound. Soybeans gained ¾ cent to $12.11 while Chicago wheat added 1¾ cents to $6.36. The grain complex is marking time through planting season — no weather premium, no demand surprise, just following energy's lead. Soybean meal held steady at $320.60 while soybean oil gained 15 cents to $75.39 on the crude oil rally.
sections.bottom_line: Grains following energy higher but no fundamental catalyst yet.

spread_to_watch.label: Live cattle / feeder ratio
spread_to_watch.commentary: The spread between live cattle and feeders compressed as both contracts fell, but feeders held better. This ratio typically narrows when the fed cattle market weakens relative to replacement costs. Watch for the ratio to break below 67 — that would signal the entire beef complex is repricing lower, not just the fed cattle market.

basis.headline: Cattle basis weakening as futures break support levels.
basis.body: Regional cattle basis is following the futures weakness with Southern Plains locations giving up 50-75 cents from last week's premiums. Corn basis is steady to firm in the Eastern Belt as ethanol demand holds, but Western locations are softening with the seasonal. Energy costs spiking add pressure to local trucking premiums across all commodities.

weekly_thread.question: Will the cattle complex find support at $250 or continue the breakdown through key technical levels?
weekly_thread.status_text: Today's break through $252 support answers the breakout question definitively — it's over. The new question is whether the selling continues through $250 or if value buyers step in. Tuesday's Cattle on Feed report will determine if the fundamental story supports the technical breakdown.

the_more_you_know.title: When Binary Levels Break: The $252 Lesson
the_more_you_know.body: Today's $253.00 cattle close, down 98 cents and through $252 support, demonstrates how binary technical levels work in practice. When a market tests a key level repeatedly and finally breaks it with volume, the break typically extends further than most expect. The $252 level wasn't arbitrary — it marked the low of the consolidation pattern that preceded the attempted breakout. Breaking it invalidates the entire setup and forces repositioning by technical traders who bought the breakout attempt.

watch_list.desc: USDA Cattle on Feed report; under 100% of last year questions the fundamental support story.
Crude oil direction on supply news; above $106 keeps input cost pressure building.
Weekly export sales; cattle under 15K MT confirms weak international demand.
Regional cattle basis; wider discounts signal cash market following futures lower.

daily_quote.text: You must be the change you wish to see in the world.

source_summary: USDA daily prices, CME Group settlement data, regional basis reports
